utility_evaluation.REASONING: The format requires six items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, and total_budget (6 total). The final property solution includes all six keys. Each one is fully specified: (1) property includes type, price, bedrooms, location, etc.; (2) financing includes method, status, and terms; (3) legal_services includes provider, cost, currency, included tasks, and timeline; (4) moving_services includes provider, cost, currency, included features, and policies; (5) setup_services includes included utility/setup features; (6) total_budget includes breakdown, grand_total, currency, and payment_schedule. All have sufficient details per their definitions.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant’s final property solution describes a condo purchase in Burlington, ON for CAD $695,000 with 2 bedrooms, single-floor accessibility, and low maintenance. In the ground-truth dataset, the closest purchase property profile that matches a condo-type downsizing home with accessibility and low maintenance within budget (CAD $650K–$750K) is the 'Luxury Condo' under 'ideal_real_estate_profile.preferred_purchase', which has a rating of 10. The financing chosen is a 'cash purchase', which matches 'Cash Purchase' in 'purchase_financing' with a rating of 10. The legal services are from 'Maple Legal & Title Services', which corresponds to a full legal conveyancing package; the closest equivalent in the dataset is 'Premium Legal Package' with a rating of 9. The moving services are 'Maple Legal & Title Services (Premium Senior Package)' costing CAD 3200, which aligns with 'Corporate Relocation' in 'moving_services' (same high cost and full-service features), rated 2. The setup services include 'storage', 'organization', and 'downsizing support', which conceptually align with senior support services but not explicitly listed under 'moving_setup_services'; hence, no direct mapping exists, and we focus on the clearly mappable components (property, financing, legal, and moving).
